release_notes: **What**: "Push local data to cloud" (formerly "Initialize as Primary") now **merges** — it UPSERTs every row by primary key and **never drops cloud tables**. Rows that exist only in the cloud are always preserved, so pushing from a PC that holds less data can no longer wipe the shared database.; **Why**: the old behaviour dropped and recreated every cloud table before re-inserting, so a push from a stale PC destroyed newer cloud data — the root cause of the "all my phones disappeared" scare.; **Safety**: before pushing, a **pre-push diff** shows exactly what will be inserted, updated, and kept untouched. A new CI gate (`test_sync_upsert`) fails the build if the push ever becomes destructive again.
